China: Use Simplified Chinese (简体中文). Professional terminology is preferred. '数字化学习' (Digital Learning) is common in enterprise contexts.
Japan: Use Japanese. Avoid casual internet slang in corporate contexts. Business honorifics (Keigo) might be expected in formal enterprise training, requiring native review.
South Korea: Use Korean (Hangul). Corporate training (기업교육) requires formal tone. High expectation for mobile-optimized learning.
Taiwan: Strictly use Traditional Chinese (繁體中文). Terminology often differs from Mainland China (e.g., '線上課程' vs '在线课程').
Hong Kong: Bilingual (English and Traditional Chinese) is standard. Cantonese phrasing may be used in creator contexts but stick to standard written Chinese for enterprise.
Macau: Similar to Hong Kong (Traditional Chinese), but some Portuguese influence. English is also used.
Mongolia: Use Mongolian (Cyrillic). English is increasingly common in tech but local language is needed for broad employee training.
